items.title: Unity minister calls for wiser digital culture in Wesak Day message
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang has called on Malaysians to uphold human values, strengthen unity and progress towards a more ethical, well-mannered and wise digital society. “In today’s digital world, unity is tested not only in the real world but also on social media. (While) a single post can unite society, a slander can destroy national harmony,” he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Malaysia backs regional push for stronger military cooperation, joint drills at Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 30— Asean defence ministers today agreed to step up joint military training and exercises, particularly in humanitarian assistance and disaster relief operations. According to Singapore’s Defence Ministry (Mindef), the ministers exchanged views on expanding cooperation through initiatives such as Indonesia’s Exercise Trident Resolve, scheduled for September 2026 and involving Asean Defence Ministers’ Meeting Plus (ADMM-Plus) countries, as well as enhanced information-sharing through platforms such as the Republic of Singapore Navy’s Information Fusion Centre. The meeting was co-hosted by Singapore Defence Minister Chan Chun Sing and Philippines Secretary of National Defence Gilberto Teodoro Jr, and attended by defence leaders and representatives from Asean member states.

items.title: US-Iran talks not a definitive fix for global energy market uncertainty, says Akmal
items.summary: He said developments in the Middle East continue to warrant close attention as the geopolitical situation remains fluid and could still affect global oil supplies. Akmal noted that Brent crude prices had fallen and remained below US$90 a barrel last week, while spot oil prices stayed under US$100 a barrel. Any disruption along the route, he said, could affect global energy supply chains and destabilise oil prices.

items.title: 'True heroine': Senator praises USM graduate for viral act of cross-cultural kindness
items.summary: He said an underprivileged Chinese man had been approaching patrons and seeking assistance. Lingeshwaran said a young Malay woman, identified as Siti Zulaiqah, a graduate of Universiti Sains Malaysia, stepped forward to help. Describing Siti as an example of kindness in action, Lingeshwaran said meaningful acts did not require wealth, power or status.

items.title: PM Anwar’s Aidiladha contribution benefits 2,000 Orang Asli residents in Ulu Kinta
items.summary: Tambun parliamentary special officer Mohamad Hairul Amir Sabri said the programme was organised by the Tambun Parliamentary Community Service Centre in collaboration with the Department of Orang Asli Development (JAKOA), with the participation of the Orang Asli community. The programme involved Orang Asli community from Kampung Makmur, Kampung Tonggang, Kampung Chadak, Kampung Suluh, Kampung Suak Petai, Kampung Choh and Kampung Baduk. Meanwhile, Kampung Makmur Tok Batin Jailani Rasid said the programme benefited the Orang Asli community while strengthening ties among residents.

items.title: Public Service Dept warns action may be taken against those spreading false info about govt agencies
items.summary: KAMPAR, May 30 — Individuals found spreading videos or false information that tarnish the image of government ministries or agencies may face action. Public Service Department (PSD) deputy director-general (Operations) Datuk Mohd Shaiful Ibrahim said such action was necessary to protect the credibility of government departments, as false information could undermine public confidence in the civil service. On the programme, Mohd Shaiful said the PSD contributed five cows for the korban (sacrificial ritual), which involved about 100 participants comprising PSD staff, personnel from the Kampar District and Land Office, and locals.

items.title: Kedah Domestic Trade Ministry probes three fleet card misuse cases
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 30 — The Kedah Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) is investigating three cases of fleet card misuse and reviewing companies granted the facility to prevent abuse of subsidised fuel. Kedah KPDN chief enforcement officer Zalina Aziz said the ministry is also reassessing companies provided with fleet card facilities to ensure they are not misused or in breach of stipulated conditions. Zalina said Kedah KPDN currently has about 190 enforcement personnel across five branches statewide, but continued to face constraints in monitoring all 362 petrol stations in the state.

items.title: Man arrested after viral road rage incident in Bayan Lepas
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police have arrested a 37-year-old man following a road rage incident where he allegedly criminally intimidated another driver and made obscene gestures along Jalan Tun Dr Awang on Thursday. Southwest district police chief Assistant Commissioner Anuwal Ab Wahab said the incident occurred at 11.27am when the suspect, driving a Perodua Myvi, abruptly cut in front of the complainant's vehicle before stopping. Anuwal said police arrested the suspect following investigations into the incident.

items.title: Police confirm Perlis MB’s shotgun legally licensed, seize weapon after viral sacrificial cow shooting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah holds a valid firearms licence for the shotgun used to shoot a sacrificial cow that had reportedly turned aggressive, police said, after a video of the incident sparked debate online. In a statement today, Kangar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said checks by the district police licensing division confirmed that the firearm was legally registered to Abu Bakar. Yusharifuddin said officers had recorded Abu Bakar’s statement.

items.title: Devotees flock to Penang temples ahead of Wesak as grand floral float nears completion
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Thousands of devotees have flocked to Buddhist temples across Penang ahead of Wesak Day tomorrow, as final touches are being made to the state's main procession float. Devotees lit joss sticks and offered prayers inside the temple, while outside, teams of volunteers carefully arranged thousands of flowers that will adorn the float when it takes to the streets of George Town. Floral arrangement adviser Chin Lee Siew, 80, said preparations began on Thursday and intensified after flowers started arriving two days ago.

items.title: US warns capable of resuming war with Iran as deal remains elusive
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States warned on Saturday it was "more than capable" of resuming war with Iran after President Donald Trump said any peace deal must adhere to his red lines, including Teheran never being able to develop nuclear weapons. US sources had told AFP the deal was waiting on Trump's sign-off, but the president had made no decision after a two-hour meeting in the White House Situation Room on Friday in Washington. "Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon," the official added.

items.title: Anwar hails Tabung Haji after fifth straight Labbaitom Award win for excellence in pilgrim services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) on its achievement in receiving the Labbaitom Award from the Saudi Arabian Ministry of Haj and Umrah for the fifth consecutive time. In a Facebook post today, he said the achievement recognises Malaysia’s excellence in the management and welfare of Haj pilgrims. The Labbaitom Award is an annual recognition presented by the Saudi Arabian Ministry of Haj and Umrah to Haj operators worldwide who demonstrate outstanding performance in Haj management.

items.title: Derailed train cars cleared as Rapid KL works to restore normal services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Four train cars that derailed recently near the Chan Sow Lin station have been successfully rerailed and returned to the depot for technical inspection. Rapid KL, in a Facebook post today, said recovery works were carried out yesterday and early this morning, involving the coupling of the train cars with a specialised track vehicle to enable their safe removal. On Thursday, a train on the LRT Ampang/Sri Petaling Line derailed near the station following a fault in a track switch, disrupting service schedules.

items.title: Police verify Perlis MB's firearm licence, seize shotgun
items.summary: KANGAR: Police have confirmed that Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah holds a valid licence for the shotgun used to shoot a rampaging cow on Thursday. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said checks by the district police headquarters' licensing division confirmed it. "A police team from the Kangar district headquarters recorded a statement from the menteri besar.

items.title: Sustained, intelligence-led crackdown needed to dismantle underground vape market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Enforcement authorities must adopt sustained, intelligence-led operations to dismantle the underground market for drug-laced vape products and halt vaping among schoolchildren. Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye said sporadic enforcement alone is insufficient to tackle the black market trade, which spans covert physical networks and online sales. "It requires sustained enforcement, stronger intelligence gathering, stricter penalties, tighter control of online sales, and close cooperation among various enforcement agencies," he said.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Sabah interim special grant increased to RM1.5 billion
items.summary: PENAMPANG: The interim special grant for Sabah has been increased to RM1.5 billion this year from RM600 million previously, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the increase is a temporary measure pending a final mechanism for Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63). The prime minister said he has tasked Second Finance Minister Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan and Treasury secretary-general Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican to refine negotiations with the Sabah state government.

items.title: Missing man sparks SAR operation, found asleep by washing machine next door
items.summary: PADAWAN: A man who triggered a search and rescue operation after disappearing from a wedding reception in Kampung Sira here early today was found asleep next to a neighbour's washing machine. A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesman said a distress call was received at 4.39am, prompting the deployment of a team from the Siburan fire station. Following a coordinated search effort, villagers discovered the man unhurt at 6am sleeping beside the household appliance next door.

items.title: Civil society groups seek stronger checks in AG-PP separation bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Civil society groups have called on the government to ensure that proposed constitutional amendments separating the roles of the Attorney-General (AG) and Public Prosecutor (PP) result in an independent, accountable and credible prosecution service. They said that while the bill was referred to the PSSC in March for further deliberation, neither the committee's report nor its proposed amendments had been made public. They called on the PSSC to immediately provide its report and proposed amendments to all MPs.

items.title: Domestic Trade Ministry launches Ops Kesan 6.0 to curb profiteering amid rising oil prices
items.summary: KPDN enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the operation was aimed at ensuring traders do not take advantage of rising costs of raw materials, operations and business to increase prices unreasonably. He said Ops Kesan 6.0 would also be conducted in anticipation of the gradual impact of the global supply crisis on supply chains, delivery costs and the prices of essential goods in the country. Azman said the enforcement action was carried out in accordance with the Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act (AKHAP) 2011 (Act 723), as well as the Price Control and Anti-Profiteering (Mechanism to Determine Unreasonably High Profit) Regulations 2018.

items.title: Government focused on protecting jobs as global supply crisis persists, says economy minister
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 30 — The government is focusing on keeping economic activity strong to safeguard jobs for the people amid a global supply crisis, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said the current priority is to ensure uninterrupted supply, particularly of the country’s oil supply, to support economic activity. On geopolitical developments in West Asia, Akmal Nasrullah said uncertainty remains despite indications of a decline in global oil prices.

items.title: Anwar congratulates Tabung Haji, Amal on Saudi recognition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) for receiving prestigious recognition from the Saudi Arabian Ministry of Hajj and Umrah. This, Anwar said, reflects Malaysia's excellence in the management and welfare of pilgrims. "This success reflects the commitment, professionalism and dedication of all TH staff and hajj officials who always strive to provide the best service in managing Malaysian pilgrims," he said in a social media post today.

items.title: Four derailed LRT train carriages returned to depot for inspection, says Rapid KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Four train carriages that derailed near Chan Sow Lin Station recently have been successfully placed back on the tracks and transported to the depot for inspection and further action by the technical team. In a Facebook post today, Rapid KL said recovery works carried out yesterday and in the early hours of this morning involved reconnecting the train carriages to a specialised track vehicle to enable their safe and controlled removal from the site. On Thursday, a train on the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line derailed near Chan Sow Lin Station due to a fault in a track switch, disrupting service schedules.

items.title: Rescuers continue Gunung Batu Putih search for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, no plans to call off mission
items.summary: Its director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said about 50 personnel from various agencies remain involved in the operation at the location. He said another eight personnel were deployed to the mountain summit this morning to facilitate the rotation of rescue teams. “We have continued the operation since May 24.

items.title: Family of five escapes unhurt as suspected 'tekong darat' crashes car into house
items.summary: TUMPAT: A family of five, including a 79-year-old grandmother and her two grandchildren, escaped unhurt this morning when a car driven by a suspected human trafficking agent crashed into their home. The incident occurred at Kampung Tok Malik near Wakaf Bharu at 7am. House owner Aminah Seman said she was reciting the Quran in her bedroom when she heard a loud noise outside the house.

items.title: Kazakhstan offers to take Iran's uranium stockpile, IAEA chief tells FT
items.summary: LONDON: Kazakhstan has offered to take Iran's uranium stockpile if the United States and Iran reach an accord on Teheran's contested nuclear programme, UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi told the Financial Times on Friday. US President Donald Trump has insisted that Iran must accept that it will not have a nuclear weapon and that the uranium is destroyed. Iran has insisted on its right to maintain a nuclear programme.

items.title: Kaamatan greetings pour in as ministers celebrate Sabah’s festival of unity, gratitude and togetherness
items.summary: Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said Kaamatan is a valuable cultural heritage of the people of Sabah and has long served as a source of strength for the community. ‘Kotobian Tadau Tagazo do Kaamatan’ (Happy Kaamatan Festival),” he said. Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said Kaamatan is not only a continuation of cultural heritage, but also an important platform to strengthen unity, harmony and environmental awareness.

items.title: Govt steps up supply chain protection as job losses jump 21%
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The government will continue strengthening cooperation with various countries to safeguard supply chains, ensuring economic growth and protecting employment opportunities amid global economic uncertainty. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government's immediate priority is to keep the economy moving despite ongoing global challenges and disruptions to supply chains. He said that during the early stages of an economic crisis, particularly within the first two to three months, the government's focus is on addressing supply-related issues that could disrupt business operations and affect jobs.

items.title: Anwar announces RM1.5b interim grant for Sabah while 40pc entitlement talks continue
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced that Sabah’s interim special grant will be raised from RM600 million to RM1.5 billion next year. Anwar said the increase is a temporary measure while discussions continue on the state’s constitutional claim to 40 per cent of federal revenue collected in Sabah, according to The Star . At the event today, Anwar was accompanied by Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Huguan Siou, state ministers and local representatives.

items.title: Cabinet members extend Kaamatan greetings, highlight unity and heritage
items.summary: Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said Kaamatan is a valuable cultural legacy that serves as a source of strength for Sabahans. Kotobian Tadau Tagazo do Kaamatan," he said. Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said the festival showcases Malaysia's unique cultural heritage to the world, in line with the Visit Malaysia 2026 aspirations.

items.title: Rafizi's right to choose his own path, says PKR's Akmal
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR central leadership council member Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said former PKR deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli is free to join any political party as part of the democratic process. Asked whether he would leave PKR following Rafizi's move, Akmal declined to comment. Akmal was previously regarded as one of the PKR leaders aligned with Rafizi's camp.

items.title: Driver cheats death as runaway trailer plunges into ravine
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: A 30-year-old trailer driver escaped unhurt yesterday when his vehicle rolled forward, crashed through a guardrail, and plunged into a ravine at Km90 of Jalan Gua Musang-Lojing. Shortly after he stepped out, the trailer unexpectedly moved forward. District police chief Superintendent Sik Choon Foo said police received a report on the incident at 10am.

items.title: No plans to call off search for missing hiker, says Fire and Rescue DG
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin will continue, with no plans to suspend the search. Fire and Rescue Department director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said about 50 personnel from various agencies remain deployed to sustain search operations and facilitate the continuous rotation of teams. "Mountain search operations differ significantly from maritime searches."

items.title: Massive morning fire guts hardware, furniture factories in Sungai Buloh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A hardware factory and a furniture factory were heavily damaged in a fire at the Kampung Baru Sungai Buloh light industrial area this morning. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said an emergency call was received at 7.32am. "The first factory, a 50ft-by-200ft hardware factory, was 90 per cent destroyed.

items.title: Adults must take collective responsibility for children's online safety
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: Online Safety Committee (OSC) chairman Tan Sri Hasnah Mohammed Hashim has urged all adults entrusted with the care of children to take collective responsibility for their online safety and well-being. "All adults who are taking care of children must be responsible, babysitters, parents, aunties and uncles as well. Hasnah stressed the need for greater public awareness to protect children from online exploitation, abuse, harmful content, neglect, and mistreatment.

items.title: Minister urges SMEs to tap RM5b relief fund as 20 banks join financing scheme
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, May 30 — Small and medium enterprise (SME) entrepreneurs in the country have been urged to seize the opportunity to apply for financing under the SME Stabilisation Relief Facility (SME SRF) provided by the government to help their businesses navigate current economic challenges. “As we are aware, Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) recently announced the RM5 billion SME SRF special financing facility, which is part of the government’s efforts to ease the burden on entrepreneurs and help them address the impact of global economic challenges, including the global energy supply crisis,” he said. Among the banks involved are Bank Islam Malaysia Berhad, Bank Pertanian Malaysia Berhad (Agrobank), Small Medium Enterprise Development Bank Malaysia Berhad (SME Bank), Standard Chartered Bank Malaysia Berhad / Standard Chartered Saadiq Berhad and United Overseas Bank (Malaysia) Berhad.

items.title: Malaysia’s dry season is coming — and Bomba is preparing early
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, May 30 — The Fire and Rescue Department of Malaysia (JBPM) is stepping up preparations for heatwaves and forest fires amid expectations of drier weather linked to the El Nino phenomenon. Its director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said the department held a special meeting with all state fire directors two weeks ago to ensure this year’s preparations are more comprehensive than before. Nor Hisham said a key focus in tackling forest fires is strengthening the implementation of National Disaster Management Agency (NADMA) directive on disaster management.

items.title: Snap election looming? 'Mysterious' one-day Johor assembly sitting sparks speculation
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Speculation over an early Johor state election intensified yesterday after notices were circulated calling assemblymen to attend a special state legislative assembly sitting on June 22. The development has reignited political chatter across the state, with leaders from both sides of the political divide increasingly convinced that Johor could be heading for another snap election within months. The latest speculation comes amid growing strains between BN and Pakatan Harapan following Onn Hafiz's declaration last month that Johor BN intends to contest all 56 state seats in the next election.

items.title: He stepped out just in time: Trailer driver cheats death in Gua Musang ravine plunge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — A trailer driver cheated death after exiting his vehicle moments before it suddenly rolled forward and plunged into a ravine along the Gua Musang-Lojing road in Kelantan. The dramatic incident unfolded at Km90 of the route at about 7.30am yesterday when the 30-year-old driver stopped his trailer after suspecting a brake failure. “The driver realised the trailer was experiencing brake problems and stopped the vehicle on the left shoulder of the road for an inspection.

items.title: Fadillah: Kaamatan Festival a symbol of unity and harmony
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, in a Facebook post, said the harvest festival, observed annually on May 30 and 31, reflects the harmony enjoyed by Malaysians regardless of race, religion, or cultural background. Fadillah, who is also Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said the 2026 Kaamatan Festival theme, Harvesting Unity, Celebrating Harmony, aptly captures the spirit of cooperation among Sabahans, particularly in facing global challenges such as economic uncertainty, rising living costs, and energy and supply chain disruptions. "May this year's Kaamatan Festival bring blessings and well-being, while further strengthening unity and goodwill among Malaysians.

items.title: Fadhlina: New Women’s Reading Club to foster reading culture across generations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — The Women’s Reading Club (KPM) is set to become a platform bringing together women from all age groups, including mothers, young women and girls, who share a passion for reading. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the club, which is scheduled to be launched in conjunction with the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair (KLIBF) today, will also ensure continued access to reading materials among the public. “Today, I will launch the Women’s Reading Club at KLIBF.

items.title: Chief minister orders tighter monitoring as Penang moves to keep projects on track
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, May 30 — All members of the State Executive Council, along with state government departments and agencies, including local authorities (PBT), have been instructed to enhance monitoring of development projects across Penang to ensure they remain on track. “I will instruct the state government, all exco members and government agencies, including the local councils, to enhance monitoring of project implementation and expedite the commencement of projects that have yet to start. Chow hoped each exco member would lead departments under their respective portfolios more proactively to ensure that the state government’s development projects are implemented as planned.

items.title: Johor Umno, BN warned against complacency ahead of potential snap election
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) must not be lulled into a false sense of security by their crushing 2022 state election victory, a political observer warned. "In the 2022 Johor election, voter turnout was only 55 per cent. Fakhrul said the political equation could change if the next state election runs concurrently with the general election, which could push voter turnout to 80 per cent and bring an additional 500,000 voters to the ballot box.

items.title: Nine arrested after armed riot at Sarawak snooker club goes viral
items.summary: Betong district police chief DSP Wan Mohamad Ali Wan Malek said the suspects were arrested at several locations in Pusa and Debak between 2.30pm and 4pm. “The arrests were made following a police report on the incident lodged earlier at 11.56am. Wan Mohamad Ali said two of the suspects were found to have prior records related to drug abuse, while only one of the nine tested positive for ketamine.

items.title: Search for missing hiker Jaslinda enters sixth day on Gunung Batu Putih in Perak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — The search for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin entered its sixth day today, with 50 personnel, including mountain guides, involved in efforts to locate the 49-year-old on Gunung Batu Putih in Perak. State Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said six members of the department’s Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team of Malaysia (STORM), two Wildlife and National Parks Department personnel, five Forestry Department officers and three mountain guides had joined the latest phase of the search. Sabarodzi said rescue teams would also retrieve memory cards from trail cameras along the route in a bid to uncover fresh leads.

items.title: Gunung Lang boat services paused until June 4 for repairs, island attractions temporarily out of reach, says Ipoh council
items.summary: IPOH, May 29 — Boat service operations at Gunung Lang Recreational Park, one of Ipoh’s major tourist attractions, have been temporarily suspended from May 29 to June 4. The Ipoh City Council said in a statement that the temporary closure is to allow repair and maintenance works to be carried out on two boats, Bot Lang 4 and Bot Lang 5. The council also said boat services would resume if the repair works are completed earlier than scheduled.

items.title: US praises Malaysia, Asean for Thailand-Cambodia observer mission
items.summary: SINGAPORE: The United States has praised Malaysia's leadership in rapidly deploying an Asean observer team to the Thailand-Cambodia border, describing the initiative as a meaningful contribution to regional peace and security. Speaking at the 23rd IISS Shangri-La Dialogue today, US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth commended Malaysia and Asean for taking proactive steps to ease tensions and support diplomatic efforts, saying the mission demonstrated the region's growing commitment to maintaining stability through collective action. "I commend Malaysia and Asean for their efforts in support of President Donald Trump's historic peace deal," he said during his address titled United States' Strategy for Peace in the Indo-Pacific.

items.title: PM Anwar: Madani govt determined to strengthen development in Sabah, Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Madani Government is committed to ensuring Sabah and Sarawak receive equitable and sustainable development benefits through educational opportunities, quality healthcare, and comprehensive infrastructure development, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. He said Kaamatan and Gawai are thanksgiving celebrations rooted in the traditions and heritage of the communities in Sabah and Sarawak, observed in a spirit of togetherness, consensus and appreciation for the blessings of a bountiful harvest. Anwar said the celebrations also reflect the importance of togetherness in reinforcing national harmony.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Kaamatan more than cultural celebration, is symbol of gratitude, unity and togetherness
items.summary: In a Facebook post, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said the harvest festival, observed annually on May 30 and 31, also reflects the harmony enjoyed by Malaysians regardless of race, religion or cultural background. “May this year’s Kaamatan Festival bring blessings and well-being, while further strengthening unity and goodwill among Malaysians. Kotobian Tadau Tagazo do Kaamatan (Happy Kaamatan Festival),” he said.

items.title: Anwar renews commitment to fairer development for Sabah and Sarawak in Kaamatan, Gawai message
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — The Madani government is committed to ensuring that Sabah and Sarawak receive equitable and sustained development through better educational opportunities, access to quality healthcare and comprehensive basic infrastructure, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. In a Facebook post marking the Kaamatan and Gawai celebrations, Anwar said these efforts are aimed at ensuring that people in both states enjoy a more dignified quality of life. The prime minister said the celebrations also reflect the importance of togetherness in reinforcing national harmony.

items.title: Sabah CM: Kaamatan festival symbolises gratitude, unity and harmony of Sabahans
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 30 — The Kaamatan Festival, celebrated annually in May, is a significant cultural heritage that goes beyond tradition, symbolising gratitude, unity and harmony among the people of Sabah. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the theme of this year’s celebration, “Menuai Perpaduan, Meraikan Keharmonian”, aptly reflects the spirit of Sabah, which is rich in cultural diversity, customs and beliefs. ”Kotobian Tadau Tagazo Do Kaamatan,” he said.

items.title: Canadian poison seller pleads guilty to aiding suicides, avoids murder trial
items.summary: The details of Law's operation have caused widespread outrage since his arrest in 2023. The list of 41 countries where Law sent poison included Australia, China, France and Brazil. Jeshennia Bedoya-Lopez, who died in 2022 aged 18, was one of Law's victims.

items.title: US Defence Secretary Hegseth praises Malaysia, Asean for taking proactive steps to reduce tensions and support diplomacy
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 30 — The United States (US) has hailed Malaysia’s leadership in rapidly deploying an Aseanobserver team to the Thailand-Cambodia border, describing the mission as a significant contribution to regional peace and burden-sharing efforts in Southeast Asia. “Nations in the region are stepping forward to preserve peace, reduce tensions, and support diplomacy with credible capability. Meanwhile, Hegseth said the US has observed its partners, particularly in Southeast Asia, stepping up efforts to strengthen regional defence capabilities.

items.title: In Penang this long weekend? Ferry rides are free on Wesak Day, Agong’s birthday
items.summary: SPPP chairman Datuk Yeoh Soon Hin said the free fares cover return trips between the Sultan Abdul Halim Terminal and the Raja Tun Uda Terminal here. Yeoh said 7,127 foot passengers and 2,221 two-wheeler users used the ferry service for return journeys during last year’s Wesak Day celebration. He said the figure contributed to the ferry service’s overall statistics last year, which recorded 2,347,066 foot passengers and 784,065 two-wheeler passengers.

items.title: ‘Momogun’ versus KDMR? Sabah DCM says Kaamatan should unite, not divide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Sabah Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Seri Joachim Gunsalam has urged Sabahans to reject attempts to create new ethnic divisions, saying the annual Kaamatan Festival should remain a symbol of unity among the state’s indigenous communities. According to the New Straits Times , Joachim cautioned that efforts by some groups to redefine or separate long-established ethnic identities risk undermining the harmony that has long existed among Sabah’s diverse indigenous peoples. Joachim, who is also deputy president of the Kadazandusun Cultural Association (KDCA), noted that Sabah’s more than 30 indigenous ethnic groups share common cultural roots despite differences in dialects, geography and beliefs.

items.title: Kaamatan: Hajiji urges state to stay unified as 'one big family'
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The annual Kaamatan Festival is more than a cultural tradition, serving as a symbol of gratitude, unity and harmony among Sabahans, Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said. He said this year's theme, "Menuai Perpaduan, Meraikan Keharmonian" (Harvesting Unity, Celebrating Harmony), aptly reflects Sabah's spirit as a state rich in cultural diversity, customs and beliefs. "Kotobian Tadau Tagazo Do Kaamatan," he said.

items.title: U Mobile ULTRA5G: 390mil scam attempts successfully blocked since network inception
items.summary: Positioned as a next-generation network, it is designed to address both the data demands and security requirements of the current digital environment. NETWORK-LEVEL SECURITY AND SCAM PREVENTION A core feature of this infrastructure is Ultra Security, a built-in next-gen security function designed to protect users on the network against phishing SMS, fraudulent voice calls and flagged websites. Internal data indicates that this mobile security platform has intercepted over 390 million scam attempts since the network began operations.

items.title: A nap too long: Padawan man reported missing found peacefully asleep… next to neighbour’s washing machine
items.summary: KUCHING, May 30 — A man’s simple bathroom break turned into a full-blown missing person case in Kampung Sira, Padawan, only for him to be discovered hours later fast asleep beside a neighbour’s washing machine in the early hours of this morning. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) dispatched a team from the Siburan fire station after receiving a distress call at about 4.39am. He was found asleep beside his neighbour’s washing machine.

items.title: 'Alarm' in Asia over China build-up, US seeks regional balance - Hegseth
items.summary: SINGAPORE: There is "rightful alarm" in the Pacific over China's military build-up and the United States seeks a regional balance where no state has unchecked power, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth said on Saturday. "When we look across the region today, there is rightful alarm regarding China's historic military build-up and the expansion of its military activities in the region and beyond," Hegseth told Asia's premier defence summit in Singapore. Hegseth was speaking at the Shangri-La Dialogue, which brings together top defence officials and experts from around 45 countries.

items.title: Nepal asked to issue death declaration for Malaysian climber Hawari missing since 2023 Everest summit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — More than three years after Malaysian climber Muhammad Hawari Hashim disappeared on Mount Everest, organisers of the Malaysia Everest 2023 (ME 2023) expedition are urging the Nepalese government to officially declare him dead to help his family resolve legal and administrative matters. Hawari, a hearing-impaired climber from Penang, successfully reached the summit of the world’s highest mountain on May 19, 2023, at the age of 33. Mudita said Nepal was prepared to assist Hawari’s family.

items.title: NRD: Existing MyKad still usable, no need to rush to renew
items.summary: In a statement yesterday, the NRD said the issuance of the New Structure MyKad would only begin after its launch by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on a date to be announced later. “The NRD has set priority for the issuance of the New Structure MyKad to applicants who make appointments through the NRD Appointment System, as well as holders of Plastic Identity Cards (KPP), High Quality Identity Cards (KPT) and MyKad Version 1 (without ghost image),” it said. “Holders of KPP, KPT and MyKad Version 1 are required to gradually replace them with the New Structure MyKad within three years,” the statement said.
